why: Not a banked negative: B525 is a meta-audit/correction campaign over OTHER negatives (4 CONFIRMED, 2 SHAKY, 3 CRACKED). Its keywords ('RETRACTED', 'CRACKED', 'mistakes') are corrections applied to other arcs; its net effect is partly POSITIVE (B1 revived as a live falsifiable external candidate). The kills it confirms are banked in their own arcs, not here.

claim_killed: The abelianization matrix M can be simultaneously the isotropic propagation law and a positive one-step Stein evolution for the canonical Perron-weighted spatial metric S_tet.
kill_form: other:positivity-obstruction (indefinite Stein form; non-normal transient growth)
depth_reached: single 4x4 matrix M; exact signatures of G_iso (3,1), W_iso (3,1), W_stable on E_s (2,1); later NARROWED by B527's classification of the full dim-6 Stein-compatible metric cone (S_tet is outside it, S_aff inside)
evidence_quote: **W_iso = G_iso − MᵀG_isoM is NOT positive definite** (signature (3,1)), with the obstruction already on the spatial stable space E_s = ker ℓᵀ (W_stable signature (2,1), det < 0). *Verified via eigenvalues, independent of their leading-minor method.*
citation_chain: B527: computation found there? yes — classifies the whole cone C = L^{-1}(PSD(3)) and shows the no-go is S_tet-specific, not universal; the in-arc S_tet computation stands but does not generalize.
revival_hypothesis: The EMITTANCE/spectral face B735 — no pre-B735 negative ever consulted the spectral face; spectral data could supply the object-forced metric-selection principle that would pick a point of B527's cone (the isotropy assumption was imposed, not forced). Secondarily the bounded observer menu B733.

depth_reached: catalog frozen at 7 relations / 26 tau-power ratios (|k|<=5); 19 SM params; magnitude-matched null, 500 sets; tolerances 1e-2..1e-4, precision-tightening to 1e-5; positive control (E8 forced bin) passes
evidence_quote:
Hits surviving precision-tightening to 10⁻⁵: **zero**.

**VERDICT (per the frozen criteria): NO-MATCH.**
revival_hypothesis: The EMITTANCE/spectral face B735 — no pre-B735 negative ever consulted the spectral face; the relations detector was only ever run on the pre-B735 readout list, never on emittance-lengths/emittance-spectrum quantities. Born ledger B725-B729 could also change which dimensionless numbers count as object-owned.

claim_killed: Ghost level c=1 (class (1,0,0)) admits a realized integer witness pair (i.e., the smallest ghost level is inhabitable).
depth_reached: c=1 proved exact (all three slot discriminants negative-definite, maxima -4,-3,-3); census over levels [1,50] evidence-grade only (chat-2, NOT verified in-arc); (4,4,16)/all-hyperbolic question resolved later in B547 (inert-prime mechanism)
evidence_quote: **c = 1 PROVED here (exact, tests/test_b545.py):** class (1,0,0): all three slot discriminants are negative-definite over ℝ (maxima −4, −3, −3); h(−3) = h(−4) = 1 gives completeness ⟹ **the smallest possible ghost level.**
citation_chain: B547: computation found there? yes — the ghost scanner proves (4,4,16) at c=32 is an all-hyperbolic ghost via an inert-prime norm obstruction (u²−3v²=7, 7 inert in Q(√3)), refuting chat-2's realizability claim. The evidence-grade census rows in [1,50] beyond c=1,22 remain unverified in this arc — only the c=1 kill is banked-proved here.
revival_hypothesis: Congruence tower B734 (m003 at (2), m004 at (4) standard / (8) mod-center) — ghost realizability is congruence/trace arithmetic and the tower could reframe which levels are reachable; the EMITTANCE/spectral face B735 was never consulted by this kill.

claim_killed: Un-hideability (collapse of all Parikh-lifts to one conjugacy class) tracks the Pisot / irreducible-charpoly property and therefore discriminates the object among small substitutions.
depth_reached: 7 substitutions, lift counts 2..17280, grammar + length-6 language filter; small rows exact in-arc; the sigma (4-letter object) row defers to B535's locked value 2 (in-arc fast recompute disagreed and was set aside)
evidence_quote: Even the non-Pisot period-doubling AND the reducible a→aa, b→ab are un-hideable. Every small substitution tested is un-hideable — the property is GENERIC at small alphabet size, not discriminating.
citation_chain: B535: computation found there? yes — the sigma row's 17280→2 collapse is B535's locked C2 machinery, cited not recomputed here; but the kill (genericity of un-hideability) rests on the small exact in-arc rows, so the basis stays computed-in-arc.
revival_hypothesis: Bounded observer menu B733 — hideability is an observer/prior question, and a principled observer menu could redefine the census where a HIDEABLE example (the missing discriminator) lives; the EMITTANCE/spectral face B735 was never consulted.

claim_killed: The degree=rank height-1 promotion has sign (−1)^n — odd n consumes char(−M¹), even n consumes char(+M¹) (chat-1's Promotion-Sign Conjecture).
depth_reached: exact towers at n=3 (Lawton Jacobian) and n=4 (B80 _Jm_n4_exact); conjecture predicts (1,0) at n=3, exact tower is (0,1); n=5 sits behind the SL(5) wall as the open discriminating prediction (meridian rule says (1,2)); n=6 non-discriminating
citation_chain: B111: computation found there? yes — frontier/B111_sign_structure/probe.py computes the exact n=3 tower from the Lawton Jacobian (via B103) and n=4 from B80's _Jm_n4_exact, locked in tests/test_b111_sign_structure.py; B550's verify.py hardcodes those locked values and diffs the two rules against them, so the discriminating computation lives one hop away and transfers without extra hypotheses.
revival_hypothesis: None apparent for the kill itself (exact value-mismatch at the one fully-solved odd rank); the residual open half (the n=5 (1,2) prediction behind the SL(5) wall) could conceivably be attacked with the congruence-tower machinery of B734, and no pre-B735 negative consulted the spectral face B735.

why: Not a banked negative: B553 is a verify-don't-trust HARVEST of a collaborator's session summary — mostly corroborations of already-banked work plus new verified exact positives (metallic torsion, odd-Fibonacci cluster, charge-clock decoupling). claim_killed: The four species ARE the four forward bit-pairs of the old/new bit (structural/tunnel = the successor's bit; matter = one bit remembering its next moment, sutured at one seam) — the meditation's Station 4 identity against B532.
depth_reached: 299,999 successor pairs over a ~300k-site sequence; claimed identity fails at 181,678 sites (61%); the meditation's own map fails at 48,622 sites (16%), not 'one seam'; forward bigram shown ambiguous ((0,1)->{a,b}, (1,0)->{A,B})
evidence_quote: struct/tunnel_i = old/new_{i+1}: **181,678 mismatches / 299,999 (61%)** — structural/tunnel is NOT the successor's old/new bit. The "discovered identity" against B532 is FALSE.
citation_chain: B551: computation found there? yes — the true structure (species word = radius-1 two-sided coloring, 7 windows) is B551's banked result, which the in-arc mismatch counts confirm; the kill itself is computed in-arc (verify.py).
revival_hypothesis: Bounded observer menu B733 — one-sided vs two-sided memory is an observer/readout question and a principled menu could recast what counts as the species' memory; note only Station 4 is killed (the arc verifies Stations 6,7 and scopes 1,3,5).
